beats.narration: Radiation is a hazard no human sense can detect. The survey meter is your only sense — so know which one to trust.

payload.title: ONE CHAMBER, THREE INSTRUMENTS
payload.sub: The meter is your only sense. Pick the right one.

beats.narration: Nearly every field meter starts the same: radiation ionizes gas between two electrodes. Sweep the applied voltage, and one chamber becomes three instruments.

payload.heading: Sweep the voltage, change the instrument

tiles.s: Freed ion pairs in the chamber

beats.narration: The Geiger tube avalanches one event into a full pulse — superbly sensitive. The ion chamber reads true exposure rate, accurate even in strong fields.
Two specialists finish the kit. Scintillators detect a lost source at the greatest range. And because neutrons carry no charge, only a moderated rem meter measures them.
So match the meter to the job. A search wants sensitivity; a boundary number wants truth. Choosing wrong is how good radiographers get hurt.

payload.sub: Switch it on and response-check before you approach — never after.
